Standard Operating Procedures

Location Readiness Platform Semiannual assessment cycle — Q1-Q2 & Q3-Q4

Accounts & the basics

Applies to every role before you get into your own dashboard.

Signing in

Accounts are invitation-only — there is no public sign-up. An Accreditation Specialist, or a Region Admin inviting within their own region, sends you a one-time link.

  1. Open the invite link you were sent. It shows your email and assigned role.
  2. Set a password (6 characters minimum) and click Create Account.
  3. You're signed in immediately and taken straight to your dashboard.

After that, sign in anytime from the main login screen with that email and password. Forgot it? Click Forgot password?, enter your email, and check your inbox (and spam folder) for the reset link.

Assessment periods

The platform runs on a semiannual cycle. Every dashboard has a quarter selector — currently Q1-Q2 2026 and Q3-Q4 2026. Data does not carry forward: each location needs a fresh OP 541, OP 512, and JC 427 submitted every period.

Good to know

Comments and rejection reasons trigger an email notification automatically. Invite links do not — after creating an invite, you must copy and send the link yourself.

Location Manager

Single location

Complete the three semiannual readiness assessments for your location, and respond if one is sent back.

Your dashboard

The top card confirms your location — name, Lawson #, city/state, and the current assessment period. Below it, three status cards track OP 541 (Facility Readiness), OP 512 (Facility Safety Inspection), and JC 427 (Personnel Records), each showing Pending, ✓ Submitted, or ✕ Rejected.

Completing an assessment

  1. Click Start Assessment on the form's card.
  2. Answer every item — Yes / No / N/A on OP 541 and OP 512, or Complete / Incomplete / N/A plus training dates on JC 427. OP 541 has three tabs (Facility Review, Offsite Warehouse — only if you check the box, and Vehicle Review — one entry per vehicle). JC 427 takes one entry per employee.
  3. The progress bar tracks unanswered items. Submit Assessment stays disabled until everything is answered.
  4. Submit. Once submitted, it's locked — you can't edit it yourself unless it comes back rejected.

If an assessment is rejected

The card turns red with a ✕ Rejected label and the reviewer's reason printed underneath.

  1. Read the reason on the card (it's also posted to that form's comment thread).
  2. Click ↻ Resubmit — the form reopens pre-filled with everything you answered before.
  3. Fix what was flagged and submit again. This updates your original record; it doesn't create a second one.

Comments

Click 💬 View Comments on any submitted card to read or add to that form's thread — useful for explaining a fix or asking a reviewer a question. You can post, but only an Accreditation Specialist can delete a comment.

Corrective actions

If a section of your submission has 2 or more "No" / "Incomplete" answers, the card shows ⚠ N section(s) need corrective action.

  1. Click the warning to open the Corrective Actions panel.
  2. It lists which section triggered the flag and how many negative responses it has.
  3. Choose that section, describe what was done to fix it, and click Log Corrective Action.
Note

Logging a corrective action documents your fix for the record — it does not block or require re-submission on its own.

Area Manager

One area, view-only

Monitor every location in your area. You can read, comment, and log corrective actions — you can't edit records or reject submissions.

Your dashboard

Metrics across the top (Total Locations, Complete, Partial, Completion Rate) reflect the selected quarter. Below, the Location Submission Status table lists every location in your area with OP 541 / OP 512 / JC 427 columns and an overall badge — Complete, Partial, or Pending.

What you can do on a submission

  • Click 💬 to read or add a comment on any submitted form.
  • Click ⚠ N CA to open the Corrective Actions panel and log documentation — same panel and process as a Location Manager.
Out of scope

Editing regions, areas, or locations; rejecting a submission; and inviting users are handled by your Region Admin or Accreditation Specialist.

Region Admin

One region

Manage the area managers, locations, and users in your region, and review submissions — including sending one back for correction.

Your dashboard

Region-wide metrics and an Area Manager Summary table, a Trend Analysis comparing Q1-Q2 vs Q3-Q4 completion by area, and a Location Detail table for every location you're responsible for.

Inviting a new user

  1. Click ✉️ Invite User.
  2. Enter their email and choose their role — Location Manager or Area Manager (Region Admin and Accreditation Specialist invites are Accreditation-Specialist-only), then their location or area.
  3. Click Create Invite to get a one-time link. Copy it and send it to them yourself — email, chat, whatever you'd normally use. It isn't emailed automatically.

Sent one to the wrong person, or by mistake? Find it under Pending Invites and click Revoke.

Reviewing a submission

Same as an Area Manager — 💬 for comments, ⚠ N CA for corrective actions — plus one more option on anything still Submitted:

  1. Click the small Reject link under the form's comment button.
  2. Write the reason. This is required, and it's exactly what the location manager will see, so be specific.
  3. Confirm. The assessment flips to ✕ Rejected, your reason is posted to the comment thread automatically, and the location manager gets an email — they can now fix and resubmit it.
Scope

You can only reject submissions from locations in your own region. Locations outside it won't appear on your dashboard at all.

Exporting reports

Click 📥 Export Reports for an Excel workbook — Area Summary, Location Detail, and Trend Analysis — for your region and the quarter currently selected.

Accreditation Specialist

Full access, company-wide

Everything a Region Admin can do, everywhere in the company, plus record management and comment moderation.

Beyond the Region Admin toolset

  • Manage records — add, edit, or delete Regions, Area Managers, and Locations directly from your dashboard tables (+ Add Region, Edit / Delete on any row).
  • Invite any role, including Region Admin and Accreditation Specialist.
  • Reject any submission, in any region — not scoped to one.
  • Moderate comments — delete a comment from any thread (e.g. test data, something posted in error). No other role can do this.

Dashboard scope

Region Summary, Area Manager Summary, Location Detail, and Trend Analysis all cover the whole company rather than one region. 📥 Export Reports produces a workbook with all four as separate sheets.

Who can do what

A scope check across all four roles.

Capability Location Manager Area Manager Region Admin Accreditation Specialist
Submit / resubmit its own assessments✓———
View commentsOwn locationOwn areaOwn regionEveryone
Post a comment✓✓✓✓
Delete a comment———✓
Log a corrective action✓✓✓✓
Reject a submission——Own regionEveryone
Invite users——Location / Area ManagerAny role
Edit regions / areas / locations———✓
Export Excel reports——Own regionCompany-wide

Glossary

OP 541
Facility Readiness assessment — Facility Review, an optional Offsite Warehouse section, and a Vehicle Review entry per vehicle. ~45 facility/warehouse items plus one checklist per vehicle.
OP 512
Facility Safety Inspection — a single 32-item checklist covering housekeeping, electrical safety, fire protection, and PPE.
JC 427
Personnel Records Review — one entry per employee: onboarding documents, training records, performance review, medical records, and non-clinical/clinical competency dates.
Quarter / assessment period
The semiannual reporting window a submission belongs to, e.g. Q1-Q2 2026. Each period requires its own submissions.
Pending / Partial / Complete
A location's rollup status for the selected quarter. Pending: nothing submitted. Partial: at least one of the three forms is submitted or was rejected and needs resubmission. Complete: all three are submitted and accepted.
Corrective action threshold
A section is flagged for corrective action once it has 2 or more negative responses ("No" or "Incomplete").
Rotech Healthcare — Location Readiness Platform. Reflects the platform as of the current release; if a screen looks different from what's described here, check with your Accreditation Specialist.
